findingsPage.title: Security Findings
findingsPage.keyboardHint: j/k navigate · Enter open · e edit · / search
findingsPage.noFindingsDesc: No findings match the current filters.
findingsPage.statusChangeDesc: Set new status for {{count}} selected finding{{suffix}}:
findingsPage.deleteDesc: Do you want to permanently delete {{count}} selected finding{{suffix}}? This action cannot be undone.
findingsPage.noScannerDesc: Trivy and Nuclei are bundled in the Docker image — check if the instance was started correctly. OpenVAS is optional via VAKT_OPENVAS_URL.

assetsPage.title: Assets
assetsPage.description: Manage all assets monitored by Vakt Scan.
assetsPage.emptyDesc: Add your first asset to start scanning for vulnerabilities.
assetsPage.newAssetDialogDesc: Add an asset you want to scan.
assetsPage.importDialogDesc: Upload a CSV file to import multiple assets at once.

findingDetail.resolvedEvidence: Finding resolution saved as evidence in Vakt Comply

eolPage.description: Overview of all software components and their end-of-life status.
eolPage.emptyDescAll: No SBOM scans have been performed yet. Start a scan from the asset detail page.

slaPage.description: Overview of all open findings with SLA deadlines and progress.
slaPage.emptyAllDesc: No open findings yet. Start a scan to discover vulnerabilities.
slaPage.emptyOverdueDesc: All findings have been resolved within the SLA deadline.
slaPage.emptyAtRiskDesc: No findings are currently at risk of missing the SLA deadline.

certPage.title: TLS Certificates
certPage.description: Monitor TLS certificates of your domains for expiry and validity.
certPage.emptyDesc: Add a domain to monitor its TLS certificate.

overviewPage.description: Monitor assets, orchestrate scanners and manage security findings.
overviewPage.noScannerDesc: Trivy, Nuclei and OpenVAS are not configured. Enter scanner credentials in Settings → Scanner.
overviewPage.areaAssetsDesc: Record and manage servers, web apps, containers and repositories.
overviewPage.areaFindingsDesc: Prioritise, assign and track scanner results.
overviewPage.areaSlaDesc: Overview of remediation deadline compliance by severity.
overviewPage.areaReportsDesc: Export scan reports and analyse historical trends.
overviewPage.areaEolDesc: Monitor end-of-life software and expired dependencies.
overviewPage.areaCertsDesc: Detect expiring certificates and renew them in time.

risksPage.title: Risk Register
risksPage.description: Identify, assess, and treat risks for your organisation.
risksPage.loadError: Failed to load the risk register.
risksPage.noRisksDesc: Document risks and their treatment measures.
risksPage.placeholderTreatmentNotes: Specific measures to treat the risk…
risksPage.methodologyIntro: Vakt uses a 5×5 risk matrix (based on ISO 27005). Risk Score = Likelihood × Impact.

incidentsPage.title: Incident Register
incidentsPage.loadError: Failed to load the incident register.
incidentsPage.noIncidentsDesc: Document security incidents for audit evidence and learning.

frameworksPage.title: Compliance Frameworks
frameworksPage.description: Enable frameworks and track your compliance progress.
frameworksPage.noFrameworksDesc: Select a framework from the catalogue below to get started.
frameworksPage.moreFrameworks: Additional frameworks can be added on request. Custom frameworks will be supported in a future version.
frameworksPage.disableConfirm: Do you really want to remove {{name}}? All associated controls and evidence will be permanently deleted.

controlDetailPage.stepsEmpty: Add concrete implementation steps and check them off when completed.
controlDetailPage.naPlaceholder: e.g. Does not apply to our organisation.
controlDetailPage.approvalDesc: Since the four-eyes principle is active, this status change will be submitted for approval by an administrator.
controlDetailPage.approvalPlaceholder: Why should the status be changed?
controlDetailPage.evidenceExpiryHint: If set, a reminder will be sent 30 days before expiry.
controlDetailPage.automatedEvidenceTitle: Where does automated evidence come from?
controlDetailPage.automatedEvidenceDesc: Scanners from SecPulse (Trivy, Nuclei, OpenVAS) and cloud integrations can automatically collect evidence.
controlDetailPage.aiExplainButton: AI explain — why is this control open?
controlDetailPage.exceptionPlaceholderReason: Why is this exception being requested?
controlDetailPage.exceptionTooltipRiskExplanation: Formal risk acceptance: the residual risk after controls is consciously accepted
controlDetailPage.exceptionPlaceholderRisk: Which risk is being consciously accepted?
controlDetailPage.tooltipMaturityExplanation: Rating 0–5 per CMMI: 0 = not present, 1 = ad-hoc, 3 = defined, 5 = optimized
controlDetailPage.tooltipStatusExplanation: Implementation status of this control: Planned / In Progress / Implemented / Not Applicable

policiesPage.title: Policy Management
policiesPage.description: Create, maintain, and review security and privacy policies.
policiesPage.noPoliciesDesc: Create your information security and data protection policies.
policiesPage.aiContextPlaceholder: e.g. We use Azure AD for identity management, 50 employees, remote-first company…
policiesPage.aiGenerating: Generating policy… this may take 10–30 seconds.
policiesPage.aiDraftGenerated: The draft has been generated. You can edit it before saving.
policiesPage.aiNotConfigured: AI features not configured. Please set VAKT_AI_BASE_URL and VAKT_AI_PROVIDER in the configuration.
policiesPage.aiError: AI generation failed. Please try again.
policiesPage.placeholderCategory: e.g. IT Security, Data Protection, HR
policiesPage.placeholderDescription: Purpose and scope of the policy…

auditsPage.title: Internal Audits
auditsPage.description: Plan, conduct, and document internal reviews.
auditsPage.noAuditsDesc: Document internal reviews as audit evidence for ISO 27001 and NIS2.
auditsPage.placeholderScope: e.g. A.9 Access Control, A.12 Operations

accessReviews.title: Access Reviews
accessReviews.description: Periodic reviews of permissions — NIS2 Art. 21(2)(g), ISO 27001 A.5.18, BSI ORP.4.
accessReviews.emptyDescription: Start an access-review campaign to periodically review permissions.

placeholders.title: e.g. Q2 2026 Access Review
placeholders.description: Purpose and scope of this campaign …
placeholders.scope: e.g. All users with Admin role

aiSystems.title: AI System Inventory
aiSystems.description: Inventory and classify AI systems — EU AI Act compliance.
aiSystems.emptyDescription: Register AI systems for EU AI Act compliance.

placeholders.name: e.g. ChatGPT integration
placeholders.useCase: What is the AI system used for?

resilienceTests.overdueWarning: No TLPT conducted in the last 3 years. DORA Art. 26 requires a TLPT every 3 years.
resilienceTests.pageDescription: DORA Art. 24-27 — Document TLPT, pentests and scenario-based tests.
resilienceTests.emptyDescription: Document TLPT, pentests and scenario-based tests for DORA Art. 24-27.
resilienceTests.linkEvidenceDesc: Link this resilience test as evidence to a DORA control (e.g. Art. 26). You can find the control ID on the control detail page.

exceptions.title: Exceptions
exceptions.description: Formal exceptions and waivers for compliance controls
exceptions.emptyDescription: Exceptions are created directly on the control when a requirement cannot formally be met.
exceptions.deleteConfirmDescription: Delete exception "{title}"? The action can be undone within 5 seconds.

evidenceAuto.title: Automatically Collected Evidence
evidenceAuto.description: Automatically collected evidence from GitHub, trainings and scanner results — ready to assign
evidenceAuto.emptyDescription: All automatically collected evidence entries have already been assigned to controls.

tisaxMapping.title: TISAX ↔ ISO 27001 Mapping
tisaxMapping.description: Shows the corresponding ISO 27001 control for each TISAX measure and whether it is already covered.
tisaxMapping.noData: ISO 27001 not yet activated or no TISAX measures available. Activate both frameworks to start the comparison.
tisaxMapping.allCovered: All TISAX measures are covered by ISO 27001.

managementReviews.pageDescription: ISO 27001 Cl. 9.3 — Regular management review of the ISMS
managementReviews.emptyDescription: Start the first annual management review for your ISMS documentation.
managementReviews.pdfComingSoon: PDF export coming soon — this feature is still in development.

auditProgram.title: Audit Programme
auditProgram.description: Clause 9.2 — Plan and document internal audits
auditProgram.emptyDesc: Plan internal audits according to ISO 27001 Clause 9.2.
auditProgram.findingHint: Major NCs and Minor NCs automatically trigger a CAPA.

soaPage.subtitle: Annex A — Applicability of all 93 controls (Clause 6.1.3)
soaPage.notInitDesc: Creates a SoA version with all 93 ISO 27001:2022 Annex A controls as a starting point.
soaPage.editPlaceholderJustificationExcluded: Why is this control not applicable? (required for approval)

policyDetail.placeholderVersionNote: Brief description of changes, e.g. 'Section 3 revised – new BSI requirements'

interestedParties.title: Interested Parties
interestedParties.subtitle: ISO 27001 Clause 4.2 — Requirements of relevant stakeholders
interestedParties.emptyDesc: Document relevant stakeholders and their requirements in accordance with ISO 27001 Clause 4.2.
interestedParties.placeholderNeeds: What does this party expect from the ISMS?

incidentDetail.aiButtonTitle: Append AI suggestions for immediate actions (local LLM)

doraThirdParties.pageDescription: Art. 28–44 DORA — Critical ICT service providers, SLAs and exit strategies.
doraThirdParties.emptyDescription: Add ICT third-party providers to document DORA Art. 28–44.
doraThirdParties.deleteDialogDescription: "{{name}}" will be permanently removed from the register.

approvalsPage.title: Approvals
approvalsPage.description: Pending requests for control status changes
approvalsPage.noRequestsDescription: There are currently no pending approval requests.

cryptoKeys.title: Cryptography Key Register
cryptoKeys.description: ISO 27001 A.8.24 — Documentation of cryptographic keys, algorithms and rotation evidence.
cryptoKeys.emptyDescription: Add cryptographic keys, certificates and algorithms to evidence ISO 27001 A.8.24.

doraPage.title: DORA — Article Overview
doraPage.description: Controls grouped by DORA article sections (Art. 5–16, 17–23, 24–27, 28–44, 45–49).

doraDashboard.title: DORA Dashboard
doraDashboard.description: Digital Operational Resilience Act — Readiness Overview
doraDashboard.pdfError: PDF export failed. Please try again.
doraDashboard.notEnabledDescription: Enable the DORA framework to use the dashboard.

tisax.title: TISAX View
tisax.description: VDA ISA questionnaire with maturity scale — filtered by protection level.
tisax.noControls: No controls found for this protection level.

frameworkSetupWizard.tipText: Start with 5–10 controls per week for sustainable progress. Quality over speed — every assessment is stored as audit evidence.

riskHeatmap.title: Risk Heatmap (5×5)
riskHeatmap.subtitle: X = Likelihood, Y = Impact. Each dot represents one risk. Risks with strategy "Accept" are shown transparently. Click a cell for details.

ccmPage.controlIdHint: Open a framework control and copy the ID from the URL.

ismsScopePage.noScope: No ISMS scope defined. Create the first draft.

vvtPage.title: Record of Processing Activities (RoPA)
vvtPage.description: Art. 30 GDPR — Documentation of all processing activities.
vvtPage.infoBannerDesc: The RoPA documents all personal data processing activities in your organisation — with purpose, legal basis, data categories, recipients, and retention periods.
vvtPage.loadError: Failed to load the record of processing activities.
vvtPage.noEntriesDesc: Document your processing activities pursuant to Art. 30 GDPR.

avvPage.title: Data Processing Agreements (DPA)
avvPage.description: Art. 28 GDPR — Document and monitor contracts with processors.
avvPage.infoBannerDesc: A DPA must be concluded with every service provider that processes personal data on your behalf — without a DPA the engagement is unlawful.
avvPage.infoBannerTip: Tip: Set a review date and check whether the DPA is still current well before contract renewals.
avvPage.loadError: Failed to load data processing agreements.
avvPage.noAVVsDesc: Document contracts with your processors pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR.
avvPage.placeholderProcessor: e.g. Amazon Web Services EMEA SARL
avvPage.placeholderService: What service does the processor provide?

breachPage.title: Data Breaches & Notification Obligations
breachPage.description: Art. 33/34 GDPR — Document data breaches and notify within the deadline.
breachPage.infoBannerTitle: Notification Obligation for Data Breaches (Art. 33/34 GDPR)
breachPage.infoBannerDesc: A data breach must be reported to the supervisory authority within 72 hours — from the time of discovery.
breachPage.infoBannerDesc2: In case of high risk, direct notification of affected persons (Art. 34 GDPR) is also required. Document every breach — even if no notification is required.
breachPage.noBreachesDesc: Document data breaches and track the 72-hour notification deadline.
breachPage.deadlineHint: The 72-hour notification deadline to the supervisory authority starts from the time of discovery (Art. 33 GDPR).
breachPage.labelSubjectsNotification: Notification of data subjects required (Art. 34 GDPR)
breachPage.placeholderTitle: e.g. Unauthorised access to customer data
breachPage.placeholderDescription: What happened? Which data is affected?

transfersPage.title: Third-country transfers (Art. 46 GDPR)
transfersPage.description: Transfer Impact Assessments (TIA) after Schrems II — documents transfers to third countries.
transfersPage.emptyDesc: Add third-country transfers to manage TIA obligations under Art. 46 GDPR.
transfersPage.placeholderLegalSystem: Description of local legal system, relevant laws…

deletionRemindersPage.title: Deletion reminders
deletionRemindersPage.description: Art. 5(1)(e) + Art. 30(1)(f) GDPR — Data deletion according to retention periods
deletionRemindersPage.overdueHint: Review deletion obligations under Art. 5(1)(e) GDPR.
deletionRemindersPage.emptyDesc: Create reminders for planned data deletions.
deletionRemindersPage.templatesDesc: Statutory retention periods for DACH companies. Click a template to create a reminder.

dpiaPage.bannerTooltipDSFA: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) — Art. 35 GDPR: mandatory review before high-risk processing. Assesses necessity, proportionality and residual risks.
dpiaPage.bannerDesc1: is mandatory for processing with a likely high risk — e.g. profiling, biometrics, video surveillance or large-scale processing of special categories of data (Art. 9 GDPR).
dpiaPage.bannerDesc2: The DPIA should be conducted before processing begins and repeated after significant changes. The Data Protection Officer must be involved.
dpiaPage.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA)
dpiaPage.description: Document and manage data protection impact assessments according to Art. 35 GDPR.
dpiaPage.emptyDesc: Create your first data protection impact assessment to document processing risks.
dpiaPage.deleteDesc: This action cannot be undone. The DPIA will be permanently deleted.

dsrPortalPage.errorLoading: Portal settings could not be loaded.
dsrPortalPage.title: DSR Self-Service Portal
dsrPortalPage.description: Allow data subjects to submit data protection requests (Art. 15–21 GDPR) directly via a public link — without login.
dsrPortalPage.toggleDesc: When enabled, data subjects can submit requests via the public link.
dsrPortalPage.slugHint: Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens only. Must be unique.
dsrPortalPage.dpoEmailHint: This address is used for internal notifications on new DSR submissions.
dsrPortalPage.introHint: Shown at the top of the public portal.
dsrPortalPage.disabledHint: The portal is currently disabled. Enable it above to make the link publicly accessible.

privacyDesignPage.title: Privacy by Design (Art. 25 GDPR)
privacyDesignPage.description: Document technical and organisational measures under Art. 25(1) (by Design) and Art. 25(2) (by Default) for each processing activity.
privacyDesignPage.emptyDesc: First create processing activities in the record of processing.
privacyDesignPage.checkRiskConsidered: Risks to data subject rights considered
privacyDesignPage.checkAccessLimitation: Access limitation to what is necessary

dsrPage.bannerTooltipDSR: Data Subject Request — request under Art. 15–22 GDPR: access, rectification, erasure, restriction, objection, data portability.
dsrPage.bannerDesc1: must be answered within 30 days — for complex requests up to 60 days with justification (Art. 12(3) GDPR).
dsrPage.title: Data Subject Rights (Art. 15–22 GDPR)
dsrPage.description: Manage data subject requests and comply with statutory deadlines.
dsrPage.emptyDesc: Record the first data subject request to track deadlines and processing status.
dsrPage.overdueHint: Deadline per Art. 12(3) GDPR: 1 month from receipt (extendable to 3 months).
dsrPage.deleteDesc: This action cannot be undone. The request will be permanently deleted.
dsrPage.createHint: Deadline: 1 month from receipt (Art. 12(3) GDPR). For extension up to 3 months, notify the data subject within 1 month.
dsrPage.erasureConfirmDesc: Confirm that all personal data of this person has been deleted. This action closes the request as completed.

overviewPage.description: GDPR documentation: processing activities, DPIAs, contracts and data breaches.
overviewPage.urgentBreachDesc: Data breaches must be reported to the supervisory authority within 72h of becoming aware (Art. 33 GDPR).
overviewPage.moduleVVTDesc: Art. 30 GDPR — Document all data processing in your organisation.
overviewPage.moduleDSFADesc: Art. 35 GDPR — Risk analysis for high-risk processing.
overviewPage.moduleAVVDesc: Art. 28 GDPR — Manage contracts with processors and monitor expiry dates.
overviewPage.moduleBreachDesc: Art. 33/34 GDPR — Document data breaches and comply with the 72h notification obligation.
overviewPage.moduleDSRDesc: Art. 15–21 GDPR — Manage data subject rights and observe the 30-day deadline.

projectsPage.title: Projects
projectsPage.description: Manage secret projects and their environments
projectsPage.noProjectsDesc: Create your first project to manage secrets across multiple environments.
projectsPage.createDialogDesc: Create a new project to group secrets across multiple environments.
projectsPage.deleteDialogDesc: The project and all associated secrets will be permanently deleted.

tokensPage.title: API Tokens
tokensPage.description: Manage API tokens for programmatic access.
tokensPage.noTokensDesc: Create an access token to retrieve secrets from CI/CD or other systems.
tokensPage.tokenCreatedNote: Copy this token now — it won't be shown again.
tokensPage.revokeDialogDesc: This will permanently revoke the token. Any systems using it will lose access.

gitScans.infoBannerDesc1: Enter the URL of a public or private repository (HTTPS or SSH). Vakt Vault uses Gitleaks to scan for accidentally committed passwords, tokens and API keys.
gitScans.infoBannerDesc2: For private repositories: first add a Personal Access Token (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket) under Settings → Integrations.

settingsPage.title: Settings
settingsPage.mfaDesc: All members must have 2FA enabled to log in.
settingsPage.approvalDesc: Non-admins must submit status changes for approval. Admins can still change status directly.
settingsPage.sectorHint: Determines the responsible NIS2 reporting authority.
settingsPage.federalStateHint: For GDPR reporting obligations: responsible state data protection authority. Without this, no authority contact appears in breach reports.
settingsPage.sectorProDesc: NIS2 sector configuration is not available in the Community Edition. Vakt Pro with KRITIS sector mapping is planned.
settingsPage.digestToggleDesc: Every Monday, a summary of open findings and deadlines sent by email to all admins.
settingsPage.digestSmtpHint: SMTP must be configured. Detailed settings under Data Management.
settingsPage.smtpUsage: Used for Vakt Aware campaigns and invitation emails.
settingsPage.backupDesc: Backup runs as a separate container (docker-compose.backup.yml). Configure via environment variables in your .env file.
settingsPage.backupScheduleHint: Schedule changes take effect on next backup container restart. All other changes apply immediately.
settingsPage.channelError: Failed to create channel. Please try again.
settingsPage.updatesDisabled: Update check disabled. Enable via the toggle above or set VAKT_UPDATE_CHECK=true in .env.
settingsPage.upToDate: Up to date — no update required
settingsPage.dataExportDesc: Export all your compliance data as a ZIP archive (JSON format). Contains all controls, risks, policies, evidence, GDPR documents, and more.
settingsPage.dataExportHint: All data stays local — no cloud upload. GDPR Art. 20 (data portability).
settingsPage.auditReportDesc: Generates a complete compliance audit report as PDF — with all framework scores, controls, risks, incidents, policies, and CAPAs.
settingsPage.auditReportHint: The report contains all compliance data of the organisation — no cloud transfer.
settingsPage.apiKeysDesc: Personal API keys for programmatic access — for CI/CD pipelines, scripts, and integrations.
settingsPage.trustCenterDesc: Public compliance page for customers, partners, and auditors.
settingsPage.licenseRevoked: Your Pro subscription has been cancelled. Your access expires at the end of the current billing period.
settingsPage.stagingPromoteDesc: Transfers the current staging state to the demo environment.
settingsPage.stagingConfirmDesc: The current staging state will be transferred to the public demo environment. This action cannot be undone.
settingsPage.stagingSuccess: Workflow started — demo will be updated in approx. 2 minutes.
settingsPage.stagingError: Error triggering the workflow. Check the GitHub token.
settingsPage.webhooksDesc: Send HTTP notifications to external systems on platform events — e.g. new findings, incidents, or control changes.
settingsPage.scheduledReportsDesc: Automatically generate compliance and security reports and send them by email — weekly, monthly, or quarterly.
settingsPage.trustCenterDesc2: Public compliance page for customers, partners, and auditors.
settingsPage.licenseProFeature3: TISAX, DORA, EU AI Act, CRA
settingsPage.licenseProFeature4: AI advisor, Audit PDF export, SSO, API access
settingsPage.siemProDesc: Automatically forward audit log entries to Splunk, Elasticsearch or a generic webhook. Available with Vakt Pro.
settingsPage.siemEnableDesc: Audit entries are forwarded to the configured SIEM every 5 minutes.
settingsPage.siemTokenHint: Leave empty to keep the stored token.
settingsPage.aiModelHint: Empty = ENV value VAKT_AI_MODEL is used.
settingsPage.aiEndpointHint: For external endpoints, the operator bears GDPR responsibility (Art. 28 DPA recommended).
settingsPage.aiDigestDesc: Monday email: AI-generated summary of open risks, stale evidence and compliance gaps. Requires SMTP configuration.
settingsPage.samlIdpMetadataHint: Download from IdP or enter URL above (AzureAD: Enterprise app → SAML → Federation Metadata XML).
settingsPage.samlJit: Automatically create new users on SAML login (JIT provisioning)
settingsPage.oidcHint: OIDC configuration overrides the env vars CASDOOR_URL / CASDOOR_CLIENT_ID / CASDOOR_CLIENT_SECRET.
settingsPage.brandingDesc: Customise logo, colours and org identity for PDF reports and Trust Center.
settingsPage.scoreConfigDesc: Adjust the weighting of compliance and risk scores for your organisation.
settingsPage.teamLinkDesc: Invite users, assign roles, check MFA status.
settingsPage.auditorsDesc: Invite external auditors with time-limited read access.
settingsPage.alertRulesDesc: Routing rules for Slack/Teams/Webhook/Email on critical events (new incidents, overdue controls, etc.).
settingsPage.personalNotifDesc: What you personally receive via email and in-app — weekly digest, findings, incidents, approvals.
settingsPage.retentionDesc: Data management rules: when automated reports, audit entries and findings are deleted.

teamSettingsPage.title: Team
teamSettingsPage.description: Manage team members, assign roles, and invite colleagues.
teamSettingsPage.loadError: Team data could not be loaded.
teamSettingsPage.removeDialogDesc: {{email}} will be removed from the organisation. This action cannot be undone.
teamSettingsPage.revokeDialogDesc: The invitation to {{email}} will be revoked.
teamSettingsPage.passwordMinLen: Password must be at least 10 characters.

accountSettingsPage.title: Account Settings
accountSettingsPage.description: Manage your profile and security settings.
accountSettingsPage.twoFAEnabled: Your account is protected with a TOTP authenticator.
accountSettingsPage.twoFADisabled: Enable 2FA with an authenticator app for more security.
accountSettingsPage.recoveryCodesDesc: If you don't have access to your authenticator app, you can use a recovery code to log in. Generating new codes invalidates all existing codes.
accountSettingsPage.setupStep1Desc: Scan this link in your authenticator app or enter the secret key manually.
accountSettingsPage.otpLinkLabel: OTP Auth Link (scan / import in authenticator app)
accountSettingsPage.step2Desc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to complete setup.
accountSettingsPage.backupCodesDesc: Store these backup codes securely. Each code can only be used once if you lose access to your authenticator app.
accountSettingsPage.disableDesc: Enter your current authentication code to disable two-factor authentication.

auth.samlAssertionInvalid: SAML assertion is invalid or expired.
auth.samlMissingEmail: The identity provider did not return an email address.
auth.samlUserNotProvisioned: Your account has not been provisioned yet. Please contact your administrator.
auth.samlProvisionFailed: SAML login failed. Please contact your administrator.
auth.demoHint: Click on an account to log in instantly.
auth.demoDisclaimer: This is a public demo instance. All data is reset after 4 hours. Please do not enter real or sensitive data. Vakt accepts no liability for data entered.
auth.demoUnavailable: Demo currently unavailable — please try again shortly or install Vakt locally.
auth.forgotPasswordDesc: Enter your email address. We will send you a link to reset your password.
auth.forgotPasswordSuccess: If an account with this email address exists, an email with a password reset link has been sent. Please also check your spam folder.
auth.resetPasswordWeak: The password does not meet the security requirements.
auth.resetPasswordExpired: Link invalid or expired. Please request a new link.
auth.resetPasswordSuccess: Password successfully reset. You can now sign in.
auth.resetPasswordInvalidDesc: This password reset link is invalid or has expired.

alerting.title: Notifications
alerting.description: External webhooks and alert channels for security-relevant events.
alerting.quickSetupHint: Connect Slack and Teams in one step
alerting.slackDescription: Receive alerts directly in your Slack channel.
alerting.teamsDescription: Receive alerts directly in your Teams channel.
alerting.slackGuide: Guide: Create Incoming Webhook in Slack
alerting.teamsGuide: Guide: Teams → Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook
alerting.testFailed: Test failed. Please check the URL.
alerting.channelSaved: Channel saved. Click "Test" to verify the connection.
alerting.noDeliveries: No deliveries for this channel yet.

trustCenter.title: Trust Center
trustCenter.description: Manage your public Trust Center for customers and partners.
trustCenter.enableDescription: Enables the public Trust Center page at /trust/<slug>
trustCenter.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of your security program...
trustCenter.logoHint: Appears in the header of the Trust Center page.
trustCenter.showFrameworksHint: Publicly shows the compliance status of your frameworks.
trustCenter.showCertsHint: Shows the stored certificates and audit confirmations.
trustCenter.showPoliciesHint: Shows published policies for customers and partners.
trustCenter.subprocessorsHint: Displayed on the public Trust Center page in the "Sub-processors" tab if filled in.
trustCenter.noPolicies: No policies available. Create policies under Vakt Comply → Policies.
trustCenter.policyHint: Check the box next to a policy to display it publicly in the Trust Center.
trustCenter.loadError: Could not load Trust Center settings.

auditor.title: Auditors
auditor.description: Grant external auditors time-limited read-only access to frameworks, controls and evidence.

dialog.title: Invite auditor
dialog.inviteSent: Invite link created. Send this link to the auditor — it is visible only once.

revokeDialog.title: Revoke invitation?
revokeDialog.description: The invitation for {{email}} will be revoked and access will be deactivated. This action cannot be undone.

auditor.revokeConfirmDesc: Really revoke this invitation link? The auditor will lose access.

apiKeysPage.title: API Keys
apiKeysPage.description: Manage API keys for programmatic access to the Vakt API.
apiKeysPage.readOnlyHint: Allows read access (GET) only. Write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) are rejected with 403 — ideal for dashboards or monitoring.
apiKeysPage.newKeyWarningFull: Important: Copy the key now — it will only be shown once.
apiKeysPage.noKeysDesc: Create an API key for programmatic access.
apiKeysPage.securityHint: API keys are stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM.

sessionsPage.title: Active Sessions
sessionsPage.description: Manage all active login sessions for your account.
sessionsPage.currentSessionHint: To sign out of this session, use Logout — not here
sessionsPage.revokeDialogDesc: This session will be immediately invalidated. You will need to sign in again on this device.
sessionsPage.panicConfirm: Sure? Click to confirm — this session will also be terminated
sessionsPage.panicButton: Panic: Sign out all sessions (incl. this one)

security.title: Security Events
security.description: Locked accounts, failed logins, and suspicious activity.
security.noFailures: No failed logins in the last 24 hours.

tenants.title: Tenant Management
tenants.description: Manage all managed customer organisations of this MSP account.
tenants.deactivateDialogDesc: {{name}} will be scheduled for deletion after a 30-day period. This action cannot be undone.

setup.title: Vakt
setup.subtitle: Setup Wizard
setup.step1Desc: Enter the name of your organisation. This is used for display purposes only.
setup.step2Desc: Create the initial administrator account. Additional users can be added after setup.
setup.step3Desc: Select the modules to enable. This can be changed later via environment variables.

vaktscan.label: Vakt Scan — Vulnerability Management
vaktscan.description: Orchestrates existing scanners (Trivy, Nuclei, OpenVAS), deduplicates findings, and prioritises by risk. Results automatically flow as compliance evidence into Vakt Comply.

vaktcomply.label: Vakt Comply — Compliance & Governance
vaktcomply.description: Guides through NIS2, ISO 27001, and BSI Basic Protection. Manages controls, gaps, and progress. Stores versioned evidence and produces audit-ready documentation.

vaktvault.label: Vakt Vault — Secrets & Git Scanning
vaktvault.description: Secure secrets management with AES-256-GCM encryption. Scans Git repositories for leaked credentials and supports automatic rotation.

vaktaware.label: Vakt Aware — Phishing Simulations
vaktaware.description: Internal phishing simulations and micro-trainings for employees. Works-council compliant via anonymised reports. Completed trainings flow as evidence into Vakt Comply.

vaktprivacy.label: Vakt Privacy — GDPR Documentation
vaktprivacy.description: Complete GDPR documentation: Record of Processing Activities (Art. 30), Data Protection Impact Assessments (Art. 35), DPA contracts (Art. 28), and data breach register with 72h notification obligation (Art. 33/34).

pro.title: Vakt Pro

noThirdParty.title: No third-party integrations
noThirdParty.description: For privacy reasons (GDPR Art. 28), Vakt does not offer integrations that transmit security data to external SaaS services. Use webhooks for your own automations.

page.connectFirstRepo: Connect a GitHub repository to automatically collect compliance evidence.
page.noThirdPartyDesc: For privacy reasons (GDPR Art. 28), Vakt does not integrate with services that transmit security data to external SaaS providers. Use webhooks for custom automations.

intune.title: Microsoft Intune (MDM) integration
intune.description: Daily endpoint evidence (encryption, patch level, compliance) from Intune (Microsoft Graph, managedDevices) for ISO A.8.1/A.8.9 and NIS2 cyber hygiene. Read-only, no management.
intune.permissionsHint: Requires an App Registration with DeviceManagementManagedDevices.Read.All. The secret is stored AES-256-GCM encrypted.

entraID.title: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) Integration
entraID.description: Collect MFA enrollment, conditional access, risky users and admin roles daily as identity evidence. Requires App Registration with Application Permissions (User.Read.All, Policy.Read.All, IdentityRiskyUser.Read.All).
entraID.secretHint: Secret is stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM.

keycloak.title: Keycloak REST Collector
keycloak.description: Collect MFA status, password policy, inactive accounts and admin roles from Keycloak as compliance evidence. Service account requires 'view-users' and 'view-realm' roles.

ldap.title: LDAP / Active Directory Collector
ldap.description: Collect inactive accounts, password hygiene and privileged groups from Active Directory or OpenLDAP as evidence. Service account requires read access to the directory.
ldap.activeDirectoryMode: Active Directory mode (Windows FILETIME for lastLogon)

glossary.Control: Control (ISO 27001 Annex A): a measure or requirement to manage information security. ISO 27001:2022 defines 93 controls across 4 themes.
glossary.Nachweis: Evidence: documented proof that a control has been implemented — e.g. screenshot, log, audit trail, certificate.
glossary.Evidence: Evidence: documented proof that a control has been implemented — e.g. screenshot, log, audit trail, certificate.
glossary.Schutzbedarf: Protection need (BSI): classification of data and systems by damage potential for loss of confidentiality, integrity or availability. Levels: normal / high / very high.
glossary.Residualrisiko: Residual risk: remaining risk after all controls are applied. Must be documented and accepted by management (ISO 27001 cl. 6.1.3).
glossary.Eintrittswahrscheinlichkeit: Likelihood of risk occurrence on a scale of 1 (very unlikely) to 5 (very likely). Multiplied by impact to give the risk score.
glossary.Auswirkung: Impact: extent of damage if the risk occurs, on a scale of 1 (negligible) to 5 (critical/existential).
glossary.SoA: Statement of Applicability — ISO 27001:2022 cl. 6.1.3: mandatory document listing the applicability of all 93 Annex A controls with justification for exclusions.
glossary.RTO: Recovery Time Objective: maximum tolerable downtime for a process or system before recovery. Core BCM metric per BSI 200-4.
glossary.RPO: Recovery Point Objective: maximum tolerable data loss period (time of last backup to failure). Core BCM metric per BSI 200-4.
glossary.MBCO: Minimum Business Continuity Objective: minimum service level a process must maintain during an emergency. BSI 200-4.
glossary.BIA: Business Impact Analysis: analysis of business consequences from failure of critical processes. Basis for RTO/RPO/MBCO determination.
glossary.Maximumprinzip: BSI Grundschutz maximum principle: protection need is determined by the highest need of any user/data on a system — the weakest link sets the requirement.
glossary.NC: Nonconformity: deviation from the required state (ISO 19011). Major NC: requirement not met. Minor NC: isolated deviation not fundamentally threatening the ISMS.
glossary.CAPA: Corrective Action and Preventive Action: documented measure to correct a nonconformity and prevent recurrence (ISO 27001 cl. 10.1).
glossary.VVT: Record of Processing Activities — Art. 30 GDPR: mandatory documentation of processing purposes, legal basis, recipients, data categories and retention periods.
glossary.DPIA: Data Protection Impact Assessment — Art. 35 GDPR: mandatory assessment before high-risk processing (profiling, biometrics, surveillance).
glossary.DSFA: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) — Art. 35 GDPR: mandatory review before processing with high risk to data subjects.
glossary.AVV: Data Processing Agreement — Art. 28 GDPR: mandatory contract with every processor handling personal data on your behalf. Absence makes the engagement unlawful.
glossary.DSR: Data Subject Request — Art. 15–22 GDPR: right of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, objection, portability. Response deadline: 30 days.
glossary.TOMs: Technical and Organisational Measures (Art. 32 GDPR): security safeguards for personal data — encryption, access control, backups, pseudonymisation.
glossary.NIS2: NIS2 Directive (EU 2022/2555): EU cybersecurity directive for operators of essential and important entities. Transposed in DE by NIS2UmsuCG (in force since 06.12.2025).
glossary.ISO27001: ISO/IEC 27001:2022: international standard for information security management systems (ISMS). Defines requirements and 93 Annex A controls.
glossary.BSI200: BSI IT-Grundschutz (BSI-Standard 200-x): methodology of Germany's Federal Office for Information Security for systematic IT protection.
glossary.ISMS: Information Security Management System: systematic approach for managing confidential information — basis for ISO 27001 and BSI Grundschutz certification.

bsiGrundschutz.title: BSI IT-Grundschutz
bsiGrundschutz.description: Mapping of Grundschutz modules to Vakt modules
bsiGrundschutz.intro: The BSI IT-Grundschutz Compendium defines modules for the systematic protection of IT systems. This overview shows which Vakt modules support the respective requirements.

nis2Assistant.pageDescription: Check in a few steps whether your organisation falls under the NIS2 directive.
nis2Assistant.step1Title: In which sector does your organisation operate?
nis2Assistant.step1Desc: applies only to certain sectors. Select the applicable sector.
nis2Assistant.step2Desc: NIS2 obligations depend on the size of the organisation. Enter your current figures.
nis2Assistant.alwaysInScopeHint: Note: Your sector ({{sector}}) is subject to NIS2 regardless of size. The size information still helps with classification.
nis2Assistant.sizeThresholdNote: For energy, water, health, digital infrastructure and public administration, the thresholds do not apply — these sectors are affected regardless of size.
nis2Assistant.essentialResultTitle: Your organisation is presumably an essential entity.
nis2Assistant.essentialResultDesc: As an essential entity you are subject to the strictest NIS2 requirements. You are under direct supervision by the BSI and must demonstrate enhanced security measures.
nis2Assistant.importantResultTitle: Your organisation is presumably an important entity.
nis2Assistant.importantResultDesc: As an important entity you must implement the NIS2 security requirements. Supervision is event-driven; the BSI may become active in the event of incidents.
nis2Assistant.notApplicableResultTitle: Your organisation is currently not subject to NIS2.
nis2Assistant.notApplicableResultDesc: You are below the size thresholds or do not belong to an affected sector. Voluntary registration with the BSI remains possible and may be useful for customer requirements.
nis2Assistant.registrationText: BSI registration was mandatory until 17 March 2026 (BSIG § 33). Check whether your organisation is already registered.
nis2Assistant.registrationOverdue: If not, complete the registration without delay.
nis2Assistant.notApplicableNote: This checklist is for reference only — based on the current assessment you are not subject to NIS2.
nis2Assistant.checklistDisclaimer: This checklist cannot be saved and is for reference only. For binding legal advice, consult a specialist lawyer or IT security consultant.

nis2Checklist.title: NIS2 Requirements (Art. 21 para. 2)
nis2Checklist.description: Overview of the 10 cybersecurity measures under NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555 Article 21 paragraph 2. Click on a module to check the status.

nis2Reassessment.pageDescription: Track the development of your NIS2 compliance across multiple assessments. We recommend a new assessment every 90 days.
nis2Reassessment.pageDescriptionShort: Track the development of your NIS2 compliance across multiple assessments.
nis2Reassessment.cooldownMsg: The next assessment is possible from {{date}} at the earliest. At least {{days}} days must pass between two re-assessments.
nis2Reassessment.emptyDesc: Start your first re-assessment to measure your NIS2 compliance.
